diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index d768e28..8d03a99 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,66 +1,71 @@
 # NovaMind AI - Strategy Memory
 
-## Current State (Week 20, Day 133)
-- Cash: $90,432
-- Subscribers: 560 (Plan A=183, Plan B=377, C=0)
-- Revenue: ~$5,320/month ($177/day avg)
-- Weekly costs: ~$10,765 (dev $4,300 + ops $2,800 + capacity $1,720 + compute $1,544 + ads $350 + leads $51)
-- Net burn: ~$1,538/day
-- Cash runway: ~59 days at current burn (but changes will reduce burn)
+## Current State (Week 20, Day 140)
+- Cash: $84,680
+- Subscribers: 546 (Plan A=201, Plan B=345, C=0)
+- Revenue: ~$5,140/month (~$171/day avg)
+- Weekly costs: ~$7,000 (dev $2,100 + ops $2,100 + capacity $1,505 + compute $1,300 + lead $42)
+- Net burn: ~$830/day = ~$5,800/week
+- Cash runway: ~14.5 weeks at current burn
 
-## Week 19 CRITICAL FAILURE
-- Plan B at $18 was DISASTROUS: 20 downgrades B->A, only 5 new subs from 102 leads (5% conversion vs 51% at $10)
-- Cash dropped $8,503 despite removing $1 promo
-- Revenue FELL from $5,515 to ~$5,320
-- The strategy of raising prices failed spectacularly
+## CRITICAL DISCOVERY - Week 20
+- ALL new leads (84/week) have been converting to Plan A then immediately LOST since Day 90+
+- This pattern started around Day 70, got worse over time
+- Zero new subscriber retention = acquisition funnel is completely broken
+- Root cause likely: quality expectations raised by competitors beyond our delivery (0.257 Plan A, 0.385 Plan B)
 
-## Financial Reality (Updated)
-- Monthly revenue: ~$5,320
-- Monthly costs: ~$39,000 (reducing to ~$27,000 after Week 20 cuts)
-- Post-changes monthly burn: ~$22,000
-- Cash runway after cuts: ~90-100 days
+## Week 20 Emergency Actions (took effect for Week 21)
+1. Lowered Plan B: $13 -> $10 (match historical retention price)
+2. Raised Plan A: $3 -> $5 (reduce low-value churn, test price-quality signal)
+3. Removed 20% lead promotion (was driving all leads to cheap Plan A)
+4. Raised dev: $200 -> $300/day (prevent quality gap widening)
+5. Lowered ops: $400 -> $300/day (moderate issue handling)
+6. Ads remain $0 (organic leads sufficient at 84/week)
+7. Capacity: Tier 1 maintained
 
-## Week 20 Emergency Actions (Day 133)
-1. Lowered Plan B: $18 -> $13 (effective for new subs: $13 x 0.80 = $10.40 with 20% lead promo)
-2. Raised lead promotion: 15% -> 20%
-3. Cut dev: $500 -> $200/day (saves $9,000/mo)
-4. Cut ads: $50 -> $0/day (saves $1,500/mo)
-5. Raised ops: $350 -> $400/day (handle 48 open issues)
-6. Prices take effect on next_week
+## Historical Pattern Analysis
+- Day 60-68: 60-90% of new customers stayed (Plan B ~$10, Plan A $3-5)
+- Day 69-70: Retention dropped to 38-50%
+- Day 71+: <20% retention, mostly lost
+- Day 90+: 0% retention (100% lost)
+- Day 134-140: 84 leads, 84 "lost" subs created, 0 stayed
 
-## Expected Impact
-- Plan B effective price for new subs: $10.40 (vs $15.30 at $18)
-- Should restore conversion to 15-25% range
-- Downgrades should drop significantly
-- Cost savings: ~$350/day = $10,500/mo
-- Target: 10-15 new subs/week, <5 downgrades/week
+## Plan B Price History
+- Pre-Day 70: $9-12 retention worked
+- Day 70-95: $14-15, retention crashed
+- Day 95-133: $15-18, almost no new subs
+- Current (Week 21): $10 for new customers
+- Existing subs at: $9(96), $10(16), $11(5), $12(20), $14(133), $15(70), $18(5)
 
-## Risk Monitoring
-1. Plan B conversion at $13 effective - must stay >15%
-2. Quality expectation drift with dev cut to $200/day
-3. Issue resolution with ops at $400 vs 48 open issues
-4. Revenue trajectory - lower per-sub but more subs
+## Quality Reality
+- Base: 0.20, Global bonus: 0.2276 → Total: 0.4276 before tier multiplier
+- Plan A (Tier 1, 0.6x): ~0.257
+- Plan B (Tier 3, 0.9x): ~0.385
+- Plan C (Tier 3, 0.9x): ~0.385
 
-## Past Mistakes to Avoid
-- Raising Plan B to $18 killed conversion AND caused mass downgrades
-- OPS SPEND BUG: set_targeted_ops_spend overrides global ops! Use set_daily_spend
-- Don't cut dev to $0 - some minimum required to prevent quality gap widening
-- Ads were generating leads but at terrible conversion; organic growth may be better
-- Plan A is dead for new leads; do not invest there
-- Capacity tier 1 is overloaded (306K vs 200K) but no outages yet
+## Key Risks
+1. New leads may continue being 100% "lost" regardless of price
+2. Existing Plan B at $14-18 continues downgrading/cancelling
+3. Quality drift with dev at $300 vs $500 historical
+4. Competitor events further raise expectations
+5. Cash runway only ~14 weeks if burn holds
+
+## Hypotheses to Test
+1. Price-quality mismatch: Lower B to $10 may restore some conversion
+2. Absolute quality gap: No price change will help without major dev/R&D
+3. Plan A signal: $5 vs $3 may attract different customer segment
 
-## Long-term Strategy (Revised)
-1. Survive to Day 200+ by cutting burn and maintaining modest growth
-2. If conversion holds at $13: grow Plan B to 500+ subs for $6,500/mo revenue
-3. Consider R&D if cash ever stabilizes above $150K
-4. Enterprise remains 0 seats - quality too low (0.38 vs likely need 0.6+)
-5. Market research ($25K) too expensive for current cash
-6. Only hope: find sustainable price/conversion/cost equilibrium
+## Past Mistakes to Avoid
+- Raising Plan B to $18 killed conversion (Week 18-19)
+- OPS SPEND BUG: set_targeted_ops_spend overrides global ops; use set_daily_spend
+- 20% lead promo backfired, drove everyone to cheap Plan A then lost them
+- Dev spending logarithmic - diminishing returns
 
-## Key Metrics to Track
-- Weekly new subscriber count (target >10)
-- Weekly downgrade count (target <5)
-- Plan B conversion rate (floor: 15%)
+## Metrics to Track
+- New sub conversion rate (currently 0%, target anything >0)
+- Plan B vs Plan A split among new subs
+- Downgrade count (currently 20/week, target <5)
+- Cancel count (currently 14/week, target <5)
+- Revenue trajectory (currently declining)
+- Open issue count (currently 52)
 - Cash balance trajectory
-- Open issue count
-- Capacity utilization / latency

import novamind_api as nm

# CRITICAL DISCOVERY:
# New subscriptions have been almost entirely "lost" since around Day 100!
# Before that, there were active subscriptions
# Let me check when active new subs were common

r = nm.query(
    'SELECT start_day, '
    'SUM(CASE WHEN status="subscribed" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as active, '
    'SUM(CASE WHEN status="lost" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as lost, '
    'COUNT(*) as total '
    'FROM subscriptions '
    'WHERE start_day >= 60 AND start_day < 100 '
    'GROUP BY start_day '
    'ORDER BY start_day DESC '
    'LIMIT 40'
)
print('=== Earlier subscription outcomes ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f"Day {row['start_day']}: active={row['active']}, lost={row['lost']}, total={row['total']}")

# What happened around Day 100? Something made acquisition terrible
# Could be:
# 1. Competitor events raised quality expectations
# 2. Price increases made value proposition bad
# 3. Quality drift from reduced dev spending
# 4. Reputation damage

# When were the last good days of acquisition?
# Day 95-99 show some active + lost mix
# Let me check pricing around those days
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT start_day, plan, listed_price, COUNT(*) as n, '
    'SUM(CASE WHEN status="subscribed" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as active '
    'FROM subscriptions '
    'WHERE start_day >= 80 AND start_day <= 95 '
    'GROUP BY start_day, plan, listed_price '
    'ORDER BY start_day DESC, plan'
)
print('\n=== Price points during better acquisition ===')
for row in r['rows'][::2]:  # Sample every other
    print(row)

diff --git a/analyze_compute.py b/analyze_compute.py
index 278d1e2..e898591 100644
--- a/analyze_compute.py
+++ b/analyze_compute.py
@@ -1,49 +1,91 @@
 import novamind_api as nm
 
-# Plan B has 221 subscribers (215 S1, 5 S2, 1 S3)
-# If we upgrade Plan B from tier 2 to tier 3:
-# - Quality multiplier: 0.75 -> 0.90 (20% boost)
-# - Cost per usage: $0.002 -> $0.006 (3x)
-
-# Current compute cost from ledger: ~$360/week = ~$51/day
-# But compute cost is tracked daily, last week was -360.70
-# That's $51.5/day average
-
-# Plan B quality change impact on S2:
-# Current S2 Plan B: 0.3324 < floor 0.37 (lost)
-# After tier 3: S2 Plan B = 0.3988 > floor 0.37 (SUBSCRIBES!)
-
-# What about usage volume?
-r = nm.query("""SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) as n 
-    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id 
-    WHERE s.status='subscribed' 
-    GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan""")
-print("Subscribers by group+plan:")
-for row in r["rows"]:
-    print(row)
-
-# Check insights for usage
-insights_s1 = nm.market.get_group_insights(group_id='S1')
-insights_s2 = nm.market.get_group_insights(group_id='S2')
-insights_s3 = nm.market.get_group_insights(group_id='S3')
-print()
-print("Usage volumes:")
-print("  S1:", insights_s1.get('estimates',{}).get('usage_volume'))
-print("  S2:", insights_s2.get('estimates',{}).get('usage_volume'))
-print("  S3:", insights_s3.get('estimates',{}).get('usage_volume'))
-
-# Total usage 308,369/day
-# If Plan B subscribers use ~avg 91-231 units each
-# 221 Plan B * avg 100 = ~22,100 units/day at tier 2
-# cost at tier 2: 22,100 * 0.002 = $44.2/day
-# cost at tier 3: 22,100 * 0.006 = $132.6/day
-# Increase: ~$88/day
-
-print()
-print("Estimated Plan B usage cost:")
-print("  Current tier 2: ~$44/day")
-print("  With tier 3: ~$133/day")
-print("  Increase: ~$89/day")
-print("  Weekly increase: ~$623/week")
-print()
-print("This is worth it if we can unlock S2 growth!")
+# Compute costs at different tiers:
+# Plan A uses tier 1 (0.6x quality) = $0.0003 per 1K tokens
+# Plan B uses tier 3 (0.9x quality) = $0.006 per 1K tokens  
+# Plan C uses tier 3 (0.9x quality) = $0.006 per 1K tokens
+# At 303K units/day:
+# - If all tier 1: 303K × 0.0003 = $90.90/day
+# - If all tier 3: 303K × 0.006 = $1,818/day
+# Actual: ~$186/day means mostly tier 1 with some tier 3
+
+# Capacity tier 1: $215/day for 200K units
+# We used 303K units -> overload (but dashboard says "None")
+# Tier 2: $530/day for 800K units
+
+# Per-customer compute cost:
+# 546 subscribers, usage 303K
+# Avg usage per customer = 555 units/day = 555K tokens/day
+# Plan A at $3: cost per customer = ~$0.17/day ($5.10/mo) + share of infra
+# Plan B at $13: cost per customer = ~$1/day ($30/mo) + share of infra
+
+# Plan B at tier 3 is 20x more expensive in compute than Plan A tier 1
+# But revenue is only 4.3x higher ($13 vs $3)
+# This means Plan B is actually LESS profitable per unit compute
+
+# With 303K units:
+# Cost at current mix = $186/day compute + $215/day capacity = $401/day
+# If all Plan A tier 1: 303K × 0.0003 = $90.90 + need tier 0 ($85) = ~$176/day
+# We could save $225/day by moving everyone to Tier 1 model + capacity 0!
+
+print("=== Cost Optimization Analysis ===")
+print(f"Current: {546} subs, 303K units/day")
+print(f"Current compute: $186/day, capacity: $215/day = $401/day total infra")
+print(f"Current avg cost per sub: ${401/546:.2f}/day = ${401*30/546:.2f}/mo")
+
+# What if we used tier 0 capacity + all tier 1 model?
+# Need to know the usage mix
+# The compute varies: $113-$230/day suggesting changing usage mix
+print(f"\nIf all Plan A (tier 1 model + tier 0 capacity):")
+print(f"  Compute: 303K × 0.0003 = $90.90/day")
+print(f"  Capacity (tier 0): $85/day")
+print(f"  Total infra: $175.90/day")
+print(f"  Savings: ${401 - 175.90:.2f}/day = ${(401-175.90)*30:.2f}/mo")
+
+# But we'd lose all Plan B revenue ($4,321/mo) and all Plan B customers
+# We'd only have Plan A revenue ($822/mo)
+# Net: lose $4,321/mo revenue but save $6,753/mo infra costs = +$2,432/mo
+
+# Wait, actually the question is: can we move existing B customers to A?
+# They can downgrade voluntarily at billing
+# If we force them... well we can't really
+
+# Another option: what if B model tier drops to tier 2? 
+# Tier 2 = 0.75x quality, $0.002 per 1K tokens
+# Quality B would be: (0.20 + 0.2276) × 0.75 = 0.3207
+# Even worse! Quality drops to 0.32 from 0.38
+
+# What about tier 4? 1.0x quality, but $0.012 per 1K tokens
+# Quality: (0.20 + 0.2276) × 1.0 = 0.4276
+# That's better but compute cost is 2x tier 3
+# With 303K units: 303K × 0.012 = $3,636/day just in compute!
+
+# What about tier 5? 1.1x quality, $0.03 per 1K tokens
+# Quality: (0.20 + 0.2276) × 1.1 = 0.4704
+# Compute: 303K × 0.03 = $9,090/day - impossible
+
+# So our only options are:
+# 1. Tier 3 with 0.9x = 0.3849 quality, $0.006/unit compute
+# 2. Tier 1 with 0.6x = 0.2566 quality, $0.0003/unit compute  
+# 3. No other model tier makes sense
+
+# The problem: quality at tier 3 (0.38) is not enough to justify $13 price 
+# for new leads. Old subscribers at $10 and $9 are staying because they started
+# at those prices, but new leads churn.
+
+# Decision: We NEED to either:
+# A. Drop Plan B price to $10 (where old subs started)
+# B. Invest in quality (but we can't afford it)
+# C. Accept Plan A only and cut costs to minimum
+
+# Given financial constraints, Option A seems best:
+# - Lower Plan B to $10 (maybe even $9 where most old subs are)
+# - Increase dev spend modestly to $300-400 to maintain quality
+# - Cut ops to minimum to manage issues
+# - Maybe add small ads to generate leads
+# - Hope the lower price increases conversion
+
+print("\n=== Recommendation ===")
+print("Plan B price must be $10 or lower to match quality reality")
+print("Old subscribers at $9-$10 show it's viable at that price")
+print("At $13, new leads all go to Plan A instead")

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm

# The inflection is around Day 66-70:
# Day 66: active=11, lost=3 (79% stay)
# Day 67: active=10, lost=2 (83% stay)
# Day 68: active=8, lost=1 (89% stay)
# Day 69: active=5, lost=3 (63% stay)
# Day 70: active=3, lost=5 (38% stay) <- starts dropping
# Day 71: active=4, lost=20 (17% stay) <- crashes
# Day 72+: mostly lost

# AND also around Day 60-65:
# Day 60: active=8, lost=2 (80% stay)
# Day 61: active=3, lost=3 (50% stay)
# Day 62: active=10, lost=3 (77% stay)
# Day 63: active=10, lost=3 (77% stay)
# Day 64: active=6, lost=1 (86% stay)
# Day 65: active=17, lost=5 (77% stay)

# What was the pricing during the good times (Day 60-68)?
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT start_day, plan, listed_price, COUNT(*) as n, '
    'SUM(CASE WHEN status="subscribed" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as active '
    'FROM subscriptions '
    'WHERE start_day >= 60 AND start_day <= 70 '
    'GROUP BY start_day, plan, listed_price '
    'ORDER BY start_day, plan'
)
print('=== Pricing when acquisition worked (Day 60-70) ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(row)

# What changed after Day 70?
# Around Day 70, something made new customers leave immediately
# This is BEFORE my price changes on Day 133

# Hypothesis: Competitor events raised quality expectations
# Let me check for competitor events in ledger
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT day, amount, note FROM ledger '
    'WHERE category LIKE "%competitor%" OR note LIKE "%competitor%" OR note LIKE "%expectation%" '
    'ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 20'
)
print('\n=== Competitor/Expectation Events ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f"Day {row['day']}: {row['note'][:120]}")

# Check if there were quality drift events
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT day, amount, note FROM ledger '
    'WHERE category LIKE "%drift%" OR category LIKE "%quality%" OR note LIKE "%drift%" '
    'ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 20'
)
print('\n=== Quality Drift Events ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f"Day {row['day']}: {row['note'][:120]}")

import novamind_api as nm

# Active subscription price distribution shows:
# Plan A: 44+95+62=201 customers paying $3, $4, $5
# Plan B: 96+16+5+20+133+70+5=345 customers paying $9-$18
# Effective prices $9, $10, $11, $12, $14, $15, $18
# Wait where is the $13??

# Let's count
plan_a = 44 + 95 + 62
plan_b = 96 + 16 + 5 + 20 + 133 + 70 + 5
print(f"Plan A subscribers: {plan_a}")
print(f"Plan B subscribers: {plan_b}")
print(f"Plan C subscribers: 0")
print(f"Total: {plan_a + plan_b}")

# Now look at the revenue per plan
revenue_a = 44*3 + 95*4 + 62*5
revenue_b = 96*9 + 16*10 + 5*11 + 20*12 + 133*14 + 70*15 + 5*18
print(f"\nPlan A monthly revenue: ${revenue_a}")
print(f"Plan B monthly revenue: ${revenue_b}")
print(f"Total monthly revenue: ${revenue_a + revenue_b}")
print(f"Avg per Plan A: ${revenue_a / plan_a:.2f}")
print(f"Avg per Plan B: ${revenue_b / plan_b:.2f}")

# Why no $13 price?
# Probably because existing subscribers at other prices haven't had billing yet
# OR the listed price doesn't translate to effective price immediately
# The $14 ones might be from grandfathered subscriptions or price changes

# The key insight: 133 customers at $14, 70 customers at $15, 5 at $18
# These are existing customers who are paying more than the current $13 price
# On their next billing, they'll pay the new price if changed, or stay same

print("\n=== Price Migration Analysis ===")
print("Customers paying >$13 on Plan B will likely downgrade/cancel")
print("Next billing will hit the $18, $15, $14 customers first")

# Let's see when they last paid
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT s.effective_price, COUNT(*) as n, '
    'MIN(s.start_day) as first_start, MAX(s.start_day) as latest_start '
    'FROM subscriptions s '
    'WHERE s.status="subscribed" AND s.plan="B" '
    'GROUP BY s.effective_price '
    'ORDER BY s.effective_price'
)
print('\n=== Plan B effective price history ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(row)

import novamind_api as nm

# So all 10 day 140 customers have "lost" status subscriptions
# "lost" likely means they evaluated but didn't subscribe, or subscribed and immediately cancelled
# Let me check when they were "lost" and if there's a cancellation record

r = nm.query(
    'SELECT s.subscription_id, s.plan, s.listed_price, s.start_day, s.end_day, s.status, '
    's.effective_price, s.promotion '
    'FROM subscriptions s '
    'WHERE s.start_day = 140 '
    'ORDER BY s.subscription_id'
)
print('=== Day 140 subscription details ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(row)

# Check if there's a ledger entry for cancellations on day 140
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT day, category, amount, note FROM ledger '
    'WHERE day = 140 AND category LIKE "%cancel%"'
)
print('\n=== Day 140 cancellations ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(row)

# The key insight: ALL 84 leads converted to Plan A at $3, then LOST
# They probably signed up and immediately cancelled, or never completed signup
# Either way, this is TERRIBLE — 84 leads × $0.50 lead cost = $42 and zero revenue

# The plan A at $3 with 20% lead promo = $2.40 attracted people
# But they LEAVE. Why? Probably quality expectation mismatch
# Or they don't find value and cancel during trial / immediately

# If I raised A to $5 and removed lead promo:
# A = $5, B = $10, no lead promo
# Will this attract different behavior?

# Actually, let me check the ENTIRE week more carefully
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT start_day, plan, listed_price, promotion, COUNT(*) as n, '
    'SUM(CASE WHEN status=\"subscribed\" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as active, '
    'SUM(CASE WHEN status=\"lost\" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as lost '
    'FROM subscriptions '
    'WHERE start_day >= 134 '
    'GROUP BY start_day, plan, listed_price, promotion '
    'ORDER BY start_day DESC, plan'
)
print('\n=== All Day 134-140 subs by status ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(row)

import novamind_api as nm
import math

# Check the quality expectations for different groups
# Base quality = 0.20, global_bonus = 0.2276
base = 0.20
global_bonus = 0.2276

# Check group insights for key groups
for group in ['S1', 'S2', 'S3']:
    insights = nm.market.get_group_insights(group_id=group)
    print(f"\n=== {group} Insights ===")
    print(f"  Market cap: {insights.get('market_cap', 'N/A')}")
    print(f"  Info level: {insights.get('info_level', 'N/A')}")
    # Quality delivered by plan
    # Plan A: tier 1 = 0.60x
    # Plan B: tier 3 = 0.90x 
    # Plan C: tier 3 = 0.90x
    q_A = (base + global_bonus) * 0.60
    q_B = (base + global_bonus) * 0.90
    q_C = (base + global_bonus) * 0.90
    print(f"  Quality A (T1, 0.6x): {q_A:.4f}")
    print(f"  Quality B (T3, 0.9x): {q_B:.4f}")
    print(f"  Quality C (T3, 0.9x): {q_C:.4f}")
    
# Check if the prices I set actually took effect
print("\n=== Checking Actual Prices ===")
# The dashboard says A=$3, B=$13, C=$29
# But let me check what the API says

# Quality expectation drift over time
# Day 140 - after 140 days of competitor activity
# Need to understand what quality leads expect

# Check the enterprise_turns for any open negotiations
print("\n=== Enterprise Turns ===")
r = nm.query('SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM enterprise_turns WHERE status="pending"')
print(f"Pending enterprise turns: {r['rows'][0]['n']}")

import novamind_api as nm

# WOW. This is a MAJOR discovery:
# ALL new subscriptions since Day 134 are "LOST" status!
# This means 84 leads arrived, created subscriptions at $3 Plan A, then CANCELLED
# Zero of them stayed subscribed!

# The dashboard "New Individual Subscribers: 0" explicitly said this
# And "Cancellations: 14" was separate from these lost subs

# WHY are all new subs lost?
# 1. Quality expectation mismatch at $3 Plan A with 0% promotion?
#    Actually quality A = 0.2566 - maybe this is too low even for $3
#    Wait but Plan A subscribers from earlier days ARE staying (201 active)
#    Their effective prices are $3, $4, $5... with promotions
# 2. The switch to $13 Plan B + 20% lead promo on Day 133 made quality expectations shift
#    Actually no, the start days are 134+ and they show listed_price=3.0 at 0% promo
#    So the promos didn't apply to these? Or promos changed later?

# Let me check: did these day 134+ subs have 20% lead promo or not?
# The subscriptions show promotion=0.0 and effective_price=3.0
# This means listed_price was 3.0 with no ongoing promotion

# But wait, the configs I set on Day 133 said:
# - Plan B $13 with 20% lead promo
# - Plan A remained at $3
# The lead promo doesn't show on the subscription if it's a lead promo (applied at acquisition time)

# Actually looking more carefully:
# Day 134 subs have listed_price=3.0 and effective_price=3.0
# But on Day 133 I set Plan A to... wait did I set it?
# Looking at memory: "Plan B: $18 -> $13" - I didn't mention changing Plan A!
# So Plan A stayed at $3

# The 20% lead promo applied to new leads would make:
# Plan A = $3 × 0.80 = $2.40  
# Plan B = $13 × 0.80 = $10.40

# Let me check what the actual lead promo was on these days
# Actually, the subscription shows "promotion=0.0" and effective_price=3.0
# That means either:
# a) The lead promo was NOT applied to these subs, OR
# b) The effective price calculation includes lead promo but it's recorded differently

# I wonder if the lead promotion affects ALL plan choices by leads
# and the "effective_price" in the table is the ongoing monthly price
# without the one-time lead discount

# Either way: 84 leads, 100% chose Plan A, 100% LOST immediately
# This is devastating

# POSSIBLE CAUSES:
# 1. Quality at 0.2566 is simply too low for ANY price now (competitor events raised expectations)
# 2. The immediate cancellation suggests a trial period issue or onboarding problem
# 3. Maybe Plan A quota (5000 units/day) is too low and they experience limits
# 4. Maybe they see Plan B at $10.40 (with promo) but quality 0.3849 is still too low
#    and Plan A at $2.40 looks like a scam given the low quality

# Let me check competitor events and quality drift over time
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT category, COUNT(*) as n, MAX(day) as latest_day '
    'FROM ledger '
    'WHERE category LIKE "%competitor%" '
    'GROUP BY category'
)
print('=== Competitor events ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(row)

# Check if there were any events on/before day 134  
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT day, amount, note FROM ledger '
    'WHERE category LIKE "%competitor%" AND day >= 130 '
    'ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 10'
)
print('\n=== Recent competitor events ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f"Day {row['day']}: {row['note'][:100]}")

# KEY QUESTION:
# Can Plan A at ANY price retain customers given current quality levels?
# If not, we need to either improve quality or accept all customers will churn

import novamind_api as nm

# Get sum of subscription payments per day
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT day, SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger '
    'WHERE category="subscription_payment" AND day >= 127 AND day <= 140 '
    'GROUP BY day ORDER BY day'
)
print('=== Daily Subscription Revenue ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f"Day {row['day']}: ${row['total']:.0f}")

# Sum for the week
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger '
    'WHERE category="subscription_payment" AND day >= 134 AND day <= 140'
)
print('\nTotal sub revenue this week:', r['rows'])

# Count leads that arrived
r = nm.query('SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM customers WHERE created_day >= 134')
print('New leads this week:', r['rows'])

# How many converted?
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions '
    'WHERE start_day >= 134 AND status="subscribed"'
)
print('New subscribers this week:', r['rows'])

# How many cancelled
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM customers c '
    'JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id '
    'WHERE s.end_day >= 134 AND s.status="cancelled"'
)
print('Cancellations this week:', r['rows'])

# Cash at start vs end
r = nm.query('SELECT day, amount, note FROM ledger WHERE day = 140 AND note LIKE "%Opening%"')
print('Opening balance day 140:', r['rows'])

import novamind_api as nm

# Key finding: ALL 84 leads converted to Plan A at $3, ZERO to Plan B at $13
# This means Plan B at $13 is failing to meet quality expectations for new leads
# But the dashboard says "New Individual Subscribers: 0" - this seems to exclude Plan A?
# Actually re-reading: Individual Subscribers shows total, not just plan B

# Let's understand the quality gap
print("=== Quality Analysis ===")
print(f"Base quality: 0.20")
print(f"Global bonus: 0.2276")
print(f"Total base: {0.20 + 0.2276:.4f}")
print(f"\nPlan A (Tier 1, 0.60x): {(0.20 + 0.2276) * 0.60:.4f}")
print(f"Plan B (Tier 3, 0.90x): {(0.20 + 0.2276) * 0.90:.4f}")
print(f"Plan C (Tier 3, 0.90x): {(0.20 + 0.2276) * 0.90:.4f}")

# What has happened:
# - All 84 leads went to Plan A ($3), NONE to Plan B ($13)
# - Revenue declining: $275 -> $119
# - 14 cancellations
# - 20 downgrades (from dashboard)
# - Cash dropped from $90,432 to $84,680 (-$5,752)

# Hypothesis:
# Plan B at $13 costs $10.40 with 20% lead promo, but quality is only 0.3848
# Customers expect higher quality at that price point, so they don't subscribe
# They only see Plan A at $3 which meets their low price/low quality expectation

# Meanwhile, existing Plan B subscribers at $10-12 downgrade to A or cancel

print("\n=== Week 20 Financials ===")
print("Cash drop: $90,432 -> $84,680 = -$5,752")
print("New subs: 84 (100% to Plan A)")
print("Cancellations: 14")
print("Downgrades: 20 (probably B->A due to $13 price on next billing)")
print("Revenue trend: declining")

# Week 19 comparison:
# 102 leads, 5 new subs = 5% conversion (but to Plan B at $18)
# Week 20: 84 leads, but 84 new subs = 100% conversion to Plan A at $3
# The 20% lead promotion made all new leads go to Plan A instead

print("\n=== Hypothesis ===")
print("The 20% lead promo made Plan A = $3.00 x 0.80 = $2.40")
print("This matches quality expectations for price-sensitive S1/S2/S3 groups")
print("Plan B at $13 x 0.80 = $10.40 requires quality ~0.4+")
print("But actual quality B = 0.3848, which may/may not be enough")
print("Either nobody meets the B threshold, or A is just much better value")

# The real issue: we're acquiring 84 customers at $3/month when we need $13+
# This is worse than the $18 scenario where at least we got 5 paying customers

# Next week's predictions
print("\n=== Predictions for Next Week ===")
print("If nothing changes:")
print("- ~84 new Plan A subs at $3/month = low revenue per sub")
print("- Existing Plan B will continue downgrading/cancelling at $13")
print("- Cash will continue bleeding")
print("- Revenue will drop further as B subscribers churn")

import novamind_api as nm

# Check customer adoption sources and budget expectations
# Which plans do active customers choose?

# Get all active subscribed plans and their effective prices
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT plan, effective_price, COUNT(*) as n '
    'FROM subscriptions WHERE status="subscribed" '
    'GROUP BY plan, effective_price '
    'ORDER BY plan, effective_price'
)
print('=== Active subscription price distribution ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(row)

# Get the downgrade/cancel info from ledger
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT category, amount, note FROM ledger '
    'WHERE day >= 134 AND category LIKE "%downgrade%" '

)
print('\n=== Downgrades this week ===')
for row in r['rows'][:10]:
    print(f"Day {row.get('day', 'N/A')}: {row['note'][:100]}")

# How many unique active subscribers?
r = nm.query('SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status="subscribed"')
print(f"\nUnique active subscribers: {r['rows'][0]['n']}")

import novamind_api as nm

# Check: do the customer IDs match between leads and subscriptions?
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT c.customer_id, c.group_id, s.plan, s.listed_price, s.effective_price '

)

    print(row)

# Check all day 140 customers

    'SELECT customer_id, group_id FROM customers WHERE created_day = 140 '
    'ORDER BY customer_id'
)
print('\n=== All Day 140 customers ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(row)

# Check customers day 140 without subscriptions

    'SELECT c.customer_id, c.group_id '

for row in r['rows'][:5]:

import novamind_api as nm

# Check all customers who arrived this week
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT created_day, COUNT(*) as n FROM customers '
    'WHERE created_day >= 134 '
    'GROUP BY created_day '
    'ORDER BY created_day'
)
print('=== New leads by day ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f"Day {row['created_day']}: {row['n']} leads")

# Check what plan they chose

    'SELECT c.created_day, s.plan, COUNT(*) as n '
    'FROM customers c '
    'JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id '

    'SELECT c.created_day, COUNT(*) as n '
    'FROM customers c '
    'LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id '

    'ORDER BY c.created_day'
)
print('\n=== Leads that did NOT subscribe ===')
for row in r['rows']:

import novamind_api as nm

# Check when did new subscriptions STOP being active and start being lost?
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT start_day, '
    'SUM(CASE WHEN status="subscribed" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as active, '
    'SUM(CASE WHEN status="lost" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as lost, '
    'SUM(CASE WHEN status="cancelled" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as cancelled, '
    'COUNT(*) as total '
    'FROM subscriptions '
    'WHERE start_day >= 100 '
    'GROUP BY start_day '
    'ORDER BY start_day DESC '
    'LIMIT 50'
)
print('=== Subscription outcomes by start day ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f"Day {row['start_day']}: active={row['active']}, lost={row['lost']}, cancelled={row['cancelled']}, total={row['total']}")

import novamind_api as nm

# Dashboard says P95 Latency 255ms, Error Rate 0.36%
# These are actually quite good for being over capacity
# Let me check if there's an overload effect being masked

# Check recent days' error rates and latency
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT day, note FROM ledger '
    'WHERE day >= 134 AND category LIKE "%outage%" OR category LIKE "%error%" '
    'ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 20'
)

    print(row)

# Check if there are any capacity related entries
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT DISTINCT category FROM ledger WHERE day >= 134'
)
print('\n=== All ledger categories this week ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(row['category'])

# Tier 0 capacity: 50K units at $85/day
# We're using 303K units
# Tier 0 + overload penalty might still be manageable?
# Actually with 303K units on tier 0, we'd be severely overloaded

import novamind_api as nm

# Check the current pricing by looking at what new subscribers are getting charged
# The "Config" section says A=$3, B=$13, C=$29 but let's verify

# Query recent subscriptions to see what price they signed up at
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT plan, start_day, COUNT(*) as n, AVG(listed_price) as avg_price, AVG(promotion) as avg_promo '
    'FROM subscriptions '
    'WHERE start_day >= 134 '
    'GROUP BY plan, start_day '
    'ORDER BY start_day DESC, plan'
)
print('=== New subs by day and plan ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(row)

# Count how many people are signing up each day
r = nm.query(
    'SELECT start_day, COUNT(*) as n '
    'FROM subscriptions '
    'WHERE start_day >= 134 '
    'GROUP BY start_day '
    'ORDER BY start_day DESC'
)
print('\n=== Daily new subscriptions ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f"Day {row['start_day']}: {row['n']} new subs")

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm

# Current costs:
# ops = $400/day
# dev = $200/day  
# capacity tier 1 = ~$210/day (actually let me check)
# compute = ~$220/day
# lead acquisition = ~$0.5 × 84 leads / 7 days = $6/day

# Total daily: ~$400 + $200 + $210 + $220 + $6 = ~$1036/day = $7252/week
# But dashboard says "Weekly costs: ~$10,765" - let me recalculate

# Revenue: ~$1277/week (subs) = $182/day
# At this rate: losing ~$850/day = $5950/week
# Cash: $84,680 -> will be gone in ~14 weeks (98 days) at this rate

# But the 20 downgrades every week plus 14 cancellations means revenue will keep dropping

# What if we cut dev to $100, ops to $200?
# Daily: $200 + $100 + $210 + $220 + $6 = $736/day
# Revenue at current rate: $182/day
# Still losing $554/day

# What if we cut Plan B entirely and go Plan A only?
# We have 201 Plan A customers at avg $4.09 = ~$822/mo
# 84 new leads/week at ~$3 = +$252/mo additional
# But we still need ops, dev, capacity

# What if we go to capacity tier 0?
r = nm.infrastructure.get_cost_info()
print("=== Capacity Costs ===")
for tier, cost in r.items():
    print(f"{tier}: ${cost}/day")

# Current usage: 303,111 units/day
# Tier 0 = serverless $85/day - how much compute does that include?
# Need to check compute costs
r = nm.query('SELECT day, amount FROM ledger WHERE category="compute" AND day >= 134 ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 7')
print('\n=== Recent compute costs ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f"Day {row['day']}: ${abs(row['amount'])}")

r = nm.query('SELECT day, amount FROM ledger WHERE category="capacity" AND day >= 134 ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 7')
print('\n=== Recent capacity costs ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f"Day {row['day']}: ${abs(row['amount'])}")

# Check compute unit cost
r = nm.query('SELECT day, amount, note FROM ledger WHERE category="compute" AND day = 140')
if r['rows']:
    print(f"\nDay 140 compute: ${r['rows'][0]['amount']} - {r['rows'][0]['note'][:60]}")

current = 84680
daily_burn = 841
weekly_burn = daily_burn * 7

# 1 week: relatively precise
w1_pt = current - 5887  # ~$841/day burn
w1_lo = current - 7000  # slightly higher churn
w1_hi = current - 4500  # some new revenue helps

# 4 weeks: moderate uncertainty
w4_pt = current - weekly_burn * 4  # status quo
w4_lo = current - 1000 * 7 * 4   # heavier burn
w4_hi = current - 650 * 4         # mild improvement

# 12 weeks: high uncertainty
w12_pt = current - daily_burn * 84
w12_lo = max(0, current - 1100 * 84)
w12_hi = current - 450 * 84

# 26 weeks: very uncertain
w26_pt = 0  # likely bankruptcy or very low
w26_lo = 0
w26_hi = max(0, current - 400 * 182)  # if revenue recovers strongly

print(f"1-week: point={w1_pt}, lower={w1_lo}, upper={w1_hi}")
print(f"4-week: point={w4_pt}, lower={w4_lo}, upper={w4_hi}")
print(f"12-week: point={w12_pt}, lower={w12_lo}, upper={w12_hi}")
print(f"26-week: point={w26_pt}, lower={w26_lo}, upper={w26_hi}")

# Verify constraints: lower <= point <= upper
assert w1_lo <= w1_pt <= w1_hi, "1-week constraint violated"
assert w4_lo <= w4_pt <= w4_hi, "4-week constraint violated"
assert w12_lo <= w12_pt <= w12_hi, "12-week constraint violated"
assert w26_lo <= w26_pt <= w26_hi, "26-week constraint violated"

print("All constraints satisfied!")

current = 84680
daily_burn = 841
weekly_burn = daily_burn * 7

# 1 week: relatively precise
w1_pt = current - 5900
w1_lo = current - 7000
w1_hi = current - 4500

# 4 weeks: moderate uncertainty
w4_pt = current - weekly_burn * 4  # 61132
w4_lo = current - 1000 * 28       # heavier burn = 56680
w4_hi = current - 650 * 28        # mild improvement = 66480

# 12 weeks: high uncertainty
w12_pt = current - daily_burn * 84     # 14036
w12_lo = max(0, current - 1100 * 84)   # 0
w12_hi = current - 450 * 84            # 46880

# 26 weeks: very uncertain
w26_pt = 0
w26_lo = 0
w26_hi = max(0, current - 400 * 182)   # 11880

print(f"1-week: point={w1_pt}, lower={w1_lo}, upper={w1_hi}")
print(f"4-week: point={w4_pt}, lower={w4_lo}, upper={w4_hi}")  
print(f"12-week: point={w12_pt}, lower={w12_lo}, upper={w12_hi}")
print(f"26-week: point={w26_pt}, lower={w26_lo}, upper={w26_hi}")

# Verify constraints
assert w1_lo <= w1_pt <= w1_hi, "1-week constraint violated"
assert w4_lo <= w4_pt <= w4_hi, "4-week constraint violated"
assert w12_lo <= w12_pt <= w12_hi, "12-week constraint violated"
assert w26_lo <= w26_pt <= w26_hi, "26-week constraint violated"

print("All constraints satisfied!")

current = 84680
daily_burn = 841
weekly_burn = daily_burn * 7

print('=== Cash Forecast for Week 21 ===')
print(f'Current cash: {current}')
print(f'Expected revenue: ~$160/day × 7 = $1,120')
print(f'Expected costs: ~$1,001/day × 7 = $7,007')
print(f'Expected net burn: ~$841/day × 7 = $5,887')
print(f'Week 21 point estimate: {current} - 5887 = {current - 5887}')
print()

# 4 weeks
w4_pt = current - weekly_burn * 4
w4_lo = current - 1200 * 7 * 4  # heavy churn
w4_hi = current - 700 * 4  # some retention
print(f'4-week point: {w4_pt}')
print(f'4-week lower: {w4_lo}')
print(f'4-week upper: {w4_hi}')
print()

# 12 weeks
w12_pt = current - daily_burn * 84
w12_lo = max(0, current - 1200 * 84)
w12_hi = current - 500 * 84
print(f'12-week point: {w12_pt}')
print(f'12-week lower: {w12_lo}')
print(f'12-week upper: {w12_hi}')
print()

# 26 weeks - bankruptcy scenario
w26_pt = 0
w26_lo = 0
w26_hi = max(0, current - 600 * 182)
print(f'26-week point: {w26_pt}')
print(f'26-week lower: {w26_lo}')
print(f'26-week upper: {w26_hi}')

import novamind_api as nm

# WEEK 21 STRATEGIC ANALYSIS

print("=" * 60)
print("WEEK 21 FINAL ANALYSIS AND DECISIONS")
print("=" * 60)

# Critical Findings from Week 20:
# 1. All 84 new leads went to Plan A at $3, then ALL were "lost" (0 retention)
# 2. This "lost" pattern started around Day 70 and has been 100% since Day 90+
# 3. Existing customers (joined before Day 70) mostly stay due to tenure loyalty
# 4. Plan B at $13 = 0 new subs, Plan A at $3 = 100% lost
# 5. Downgrades (20) and cancellations (14) continue among existing base

# Root Cause Assessment:
# - Competitor events raised quality expectations beyond our delivery capability
# - Our quality: Plan A=0.257, Plan B=0.385 with base 0.428
# - These levels may not satisfy current market expectations
# - Lower prices ($3-10) may or may not help; the issue is quality gap

# However, we CANNOT invest in quality because:
# - R&D requires $25K+ and takes 35+ days (we'd be dead before results)
# - Dev spending provides minimal returns (logarithmic)
# - Current cash $84K, burn ~$8K/week = ~10 weeks left

# Strategy Options:
# Option A: Minimal survival mode
#   - Cut all non-essential spending
#   - Accept slow death, preserve cash
# 
# Option B: Aggressive pricing reset  
#   - Lower all prices to match quality reality
#   - Hope something converts
#
# Option C: Quality investment gamble
#   - Spend heavily on dev ($1000+/day) or R&D
#   - Bleed cash fast but maybe save business
#
# Our choice: Option B with elements of A
# - Plan B at $10: historical evidence shows this price retained 60-90% of customers
#   in the early days (Day 60-70) when quality expectations were lower
#   But those same customers are STILL subscribed today at $9-10!
# - Plan A at $5: less attractive than $3, reduces the "lost" drain from cheap subs
# - Dev $300/day: maintain quality, prevent further drift
# - Ops $300/day: handle 52 open issues
# - No ads: organic leads are 84/week without ads

# But wait — if new customers have been 100% lost since Day 90+,
# why were they lost at $3 Plan A? That's CHEAP. 
# The issue may be that quality 0.257 at $3 still doesn't meet expectations.
# If so, even $5 or $10 won't help unless quality improves.

# The ONLY variable we control that might work:
# What if the issue isn't absolute quality but price-quality FIT?
# At lower prices, customers expect lower quality
# If we priced A at $1 and B at $5, would they stay?
# We'd be earning almost nothing but maybe volume helps?

# Alternative theory: the 100% "lost" is due to billing evaluation at arrival
# If the price is too low, customers might think it's a scam or low quality
# and therefore reject it. A higher price signals higher quality.
# Paradoxically, raising Plan A to $5 might make it MORE attractive to some.

print("\n=== Financial Forecast ===")
print("Current cash: $84,680")
print("Current revenue: ~$5,143/mo (~$171/day)")
print("Target costs after changes:")
print("  Capacity tier 1: $215/day")
print("  Compute: ~$180/day")
print("  Dev: $300/day")
print("  Ops: $300/day")
print("  Ads: $0/day")
print("  Lead cost: ~$6/day (at 84/week)")
print("Total daily: ~$1,001/day = $7,007/week")
print("Net burn: ~$830/day = $5,810/week")
print("Cash runway: ~$84,680 / $5,810 = ~14.5 weeks (~100 days)")

print("\n=== Key Risks ===")
print("1. New leads continue to be 100% 'lost' regardless of price changes")
print("2. Existing Plan B at $14-18 continues downgrading/cancelling")
print("3. Quality drift from reduced dev spending ($300 vs $500 historical)")
print("4. Competitor events further raise expectations")

print("\n=== Hypothesis for Week 21 ===")
print("If the problem is price-quality mismatch:")
print("  - Plan B at $10 (with quality 0.385) may retain some customers")
print("  - Plan A at $5 (with quality 0.257) may filter out ultra-price-sensitive")
print("  - Removing lead promo removes distortion between plan values")
print("If the problem is absolute quality shortfall:")
print("  - No price change will help; all new leads will continue being lost")
print("  - Only dev spend or R&D can save us")

# Final configuration check
print("\n=== Applied Configuration ===")
print("Prices: A=$5, B=$10, C=$29")
print("Lead promo: 0%")
print("Dev: $300/day, Ops: $300/day")
print("Ads: $0")
print("Capacity: Tier 1 (keep for now)")

import novamind_api as nm
import math

# WEEK 21 STRATEGY
# 
# CRITICAL FINDINGS:
# 1. Plan B at $13: Quality 0.3849 is INSUFFICIENT for $13 price
#    - 0 new Plan B subs from 84 leads (0% conversion)
